title: Project card and experiment plan deterministic AI draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, evidence-boundary fill-gap for Project card and experiment plan; all items remain blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Project card and experiment plan: reject the overclaim
items.prompt: Which statement is source-bounded and corrects this AI misconception? A project plan should start with the most promising model and add users, metrics, and risks after a prototype demonstrates accuracy.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Choose the statement that names mechanism, evidence conditions, and the boundary of the claim rather than a fluent or benchmark-based overgeneralization.

feedback.correct: Correct. Define the decision, stakeholders, data authority, baseline, evidence plan, non-goals, risks, acceptance, and stop criteria before model fitting.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the overclaim. Define the decision, stakeholders, data authority, baseline, evidence plan, non-goals, risks, acceptance, and stop criteria before model fitting.

criteria.description: Selects the source-bounded correction and rejects the documented AI overclaim.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against cited sources and ensure the distractor does not normalize unsafe or overstated claims.

options.text: Define the decision, stakeholders, data authority, baseline, evidence plan, non-goals, risks, acceptance, and stop criteria before model fitting.
A project plan should start with the most promising model and add users, metrics, and risks after a prototype demonstrates accuracy.

answer.explanation: Define the decision, stakeholders, data authority, baseline, evidence plan, non-goals, risks, acceptance, and stop criteria before model fitting.

items.title: Project card and experiment plan: canonical evidence boundary
items.prompt: Complete the exact source-bounded correction phrase; the complete sentence must remain an honest beginner-to-intermediate claim.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Retrieve the short checkpoint from the correction and ensure it narrows rather than expands the evidence claim.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'before model fitting'. Define the decision, stakeholders, data authority, baseline, evidence plan, non-goals, risks, acceptance, and stop criteria before model fitting.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the technical and responsible-AI boundary. Define the decision, stakeholders, data authority, baseline, evidence plan, non-goals, risks, acceptance, and stop criteria before model fitting.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical phrase that completes the bounded source-aligned correction.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the accepted phrase and technically equivalent variants before learner use.
items.template: Define the decision, stakeholders, data authority, baseline, evidence plan, non-goals, risks, acceptance, and stop criteria [[checkpoint]].

answer.explanation: Define the decision, stakeholders, data authority, baseline, evidence plan, non-goals, risks, acceptance, and stop criteria before model fitting.
